description: The adjective 'mulu' stays near the lower end of a vertical scale. It fits a low roof and the sun near the horizon. 'raelu' (tall) handles both taller extent and higher position.
articulatory_notes: Close the lips for voiced nasal 'm' and carry the resonance through rounded high-back 'u'. The tongue tip then touches the alveolar ridge for 'l' before the same rounded vowel returns. Stress falls on 'mu'.
usage_notes: The phrase 'mulu noa' means a low position. For sound volume, 'maeli' (quiet) gives the intended quality; for a short path or interval, use 'teku' (short).

semantic_domains.physical: limited height in an object or structure
semantic_domains.spatial: position near the lower end of a vertical scale
